name: Ginger Scones
recipeIngredient: 1/3 cup rinsed, dried, and finely chopped candied ginger
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees F.
2. In a bowl of an electric mixer, combine the flour, sugar, and baking powder.
3. Cut the butter into tablespoon-size pieces and mix into the dry ingredients on low speed with the paddle attachment, or combine by hand with two knives or a pastry blender. The mixture should resemble a fine meal.
4. Stir in the ginger. Pour in the cream and stir or mix just until the dough holds together.
5. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and gently knead to form a ball.
6. Roll into a 1/4 inch thick circle and cut with a 3-inch biscuit cutter. You should get 7 or 8 scones.
7. Gather the remaining dough together, handle as little as possible, and shape again into a circle.
8. Cut out about 3 more scones and place all the scones on a baking sheet. They may be refrigerated for up to 3 hours before baking.
9. Return the scones to room temperature. Bake the scones for 12 to 15 minutes, until the tops are cracked and very light brown.
10. Remove to a wire rack. Serve warm or at room temperature.
Variation: Substitute 1/4 cup currants and 2 teaspoons finely chopped orange zest for the candied ginger.
Makes: 10 or 11 large scones.
